recipes.name: Music Tool, Not Dashboard
recipes.useWhen: You need artist-facing product energy with real personality instead of productivity-software cues.
recipes.prompt: Build a music-tech launch page by taking structural cues from the base world, interaction boldness from the spice reference, and typography discipline from the editorial reference.

recipes.name: Fashion Drop With Sound Energy
recipes.useWhen: You need a drop page, campaign, or merch moment that feels scene-aware and alive.
recipes.prompt: Start from the fashion base, inject rhythmic motion and interaction from the music reference, then push color and attitude with the gen-z pop reference.

recipes.name: Culture Archive With Hooks
recipes.useWhen: You need archival, publishing, or curatorial credibility without becoming dry.
recipes.prompt: Keep the archive structure from the base world, but use the pop interaction reference to add memorability and the editorial reference to keep the typography sharp.

recipes.name: Anti-SaaS Launch System
recipes.useWhen: You want something unmistakably not enterprise, but still coherent enough to ship as a product surface.
recipes.prompt: Use the anti-B2B references for tone and interaction, then borrow enough editorial structure from the culture-tech reference to keep the result navigable.

recipes.name: Pop Editorial Commerce Without Looking Like Commerce
recipes.useWhen: You need sell-through energy for drops or memberships without default storefront tropes.
recipes.prompt: Start with the loudest pop reference, borrow gesture and pacing from the fashion interaction reference, and keep the typography editorial and taste-led.

recipes.name: Net-Art Product Narrative
recipes.useWhen: You need a product story that feels culturally current, strange in the right way, and still usable.
recipes.prompt: Treat the base as the visual worldview, use the interaction reference for tempo, and let the culture-tech reference keep the information architecture readable.

items.title: AI Matrix
items.description: AI Matrix is treated here as a poster-derived website concept. The source is the Arte Collective poster, but the design guidance describes how to turn the art into a live browser world with depth, motion, and mobile behavior.

worldSystems.name: Club Instrument
worldSystems.reason: The web version should feel like a responsive system, not a static poster pinned to the wall.

worldSystems.name: Playable Poster
worldSystems.reason: Oversized type and one clear ritual keep the poster energy intact.

archetype.name: Club Instrument
archetype.reason: The poster language wants to become a reactive field of signals, meters, panels, and pressure-sensitive motion.

mechanics.interactionModel: Turn the full poster into a responsive signal field: pointer movement shifts grids, tap reveals diagnostic panels, and drag scrubs the title, copy, and object fragments through layered depth planes.
mechanics.mobileFallback: Collapse to one active hero slab, two stacked telemetry rails, and tap-to-cycle data states instead of precision hover behavior.

items.implementationPrompt: Build a responsive website inspired by the Arte Collective poster "AI Matrix". Ignore the room mockup and frame; isolate the poster art, keep the palette around #1f2721, #e6e6e6, and #2c3f31, use Condensed Display Grotesk (approx) for the hero voice and Pixel Mono Console (approx) for metadata, build the page as a Club Instrument surface with Club Instrument mechanics, and stage the desktop hero as the whole poster becomes a tilted hud field, with title planes, copy grids, and object fragments separated into translucent depth rails. Mobile fallback: Collapse to one active hero slab, two stacked telemetry rails, and tap-to-cycle data states instead of precision hover behavior.
avoidIf: you need conservative enterprise trust cues
you need dense productivity UI
you need proof of a real live site instead of a poster-derived concept

items.title: Artifact
items.description: Artifact is treated here as a poster-derived website concept. The source is the Arte Collective poster, but the design guidance describes how to turn the art into a live browser world with depth, motion, and mobile behavior.

worldSystems.name: Luxury Archive
worldSystems.reason: The poster reads like an object accessioned into a high-contrast digital chamber.

worldSystems.name: Fan Shrine
worldSystems.reason: The browser version should feel collectible and tactile rather than informational.

archetype.name: Spatial Exhibition World
archetype.reason: The poster wants to become one hero object inside a shallow 3D room with hotspot detail and object focus.

mechanics.interactionModel: Explode the full poster across the viewport: the headline becomes architecture, the background copy becomes the room, and the hardware collage becomes floating evidence layers that react to scroll, drag, and hover.
mechanics.mobileFallback: Reduce to one guided poster stage with swipe between viewpoints and a single expandable evidence rail under the hero object.

items.implementationPrompt: Build a responsive website inspired by the Arte Collective poster "Artifact". Ignore the room mockup and frame; isolate the poster art, keep the palette around #111010, #e7e8e7, and #876541, use Ultra Condensed Grotesk Display (approx) for the hero voice and Bitmap Mono Caption (approx) for metadata, build the page as a Luxury Archive surface with Spatial Exhibition World mechanics, and stage the desktop hero as the full poster fills the room; title planes become architecture, copy becomes atmosphere, and the object collage separates into floating evidence layers. Mobile fallback: Reduce to one guided poster stage with swipe between viewpoints and a single expandable evidence rail under the hero object.
avoidIf: you need proof of a real live site instead of a poster-derived concept

items.title: Ascend
items.description: Ascend is treated here as a poster-derived website concept. The source is the Arte Collective poster, but the design guidance describes how to turn the art into a live browser world with depth, motion, and mobile behavior.

worldSystems.name: Playable Poster
worldSystems.reason: The poster already behaves like a campaign artifact and should stay singular on the web.

worldSystems.name: Luxury Archive
worldSystems.reason: The atmosphere needs enough structure to stay legible while drifting.

archetype.name: Spatial Exhibition World
archetype.reason: The poster becomes strongest as a layered atmospheric chamber with shallow depth, orbiting labels, and slow camera travel.

mechanics.interactionModel: Treat the full poster like a luminous atmospheric field; scroll advances chapters, pointer drift shifts parallax layers, and tap opens captions floating inside the composition.
mechanics.mobileFallback: Split the atmosphere into vertical scenes with one parallax hero object and one caption rail per section.

items.implementationPrompt: Build a responsive website inspired by the Arte Collective poster "Ascend". Ignore the room mockup and frame; isolate the poster art, keep the palette around #121010, #e6e6e6, and #a5886d, use Wide Grotesk Display (approx) for the hero voice and Light Grotesk Text (approx) for metadata, build the page as a Playable Poster surface with Spatial Exhibition World mechanics, and stage the desktop hero as the full poster becomes the atmosphere, with title and texture layers drifting at different depths and slim orbital captions around the edges. Mobile fallback: Split the atmosphere into vertical scenes with one parallax hero object and one caption rail per section.

items.title: Cloud
items.description: Cloud is treated here as a poster-derived website concept. The source is the Arte Collective poster, but the design guidance describes how to turn the art into a live browser world with depth, motion, and mobile behavior.

worldSystems.name: Playable Poster
worldSystems.reason: The poster already behaves like a campaign artifact and should stay singular on the web.

worldSystems.name: Luxury Archive
worldSystems.reason: The atmosphere needs enough structure to stay legible while drifting.

archetype.name: Spatial Exhibition World
archetype.reason: The poster becomes strongest as a layered atmospheric chamber with shallow depth, orbiting labels, and slow camera travel.

mechanics.interactionModel: Treat the full poster like a luminous atmospheric field; scroll advances chapters, pointer drift shifts parallax layers, and tap opens captions floating inside the composition.
mechanics.mobileFallback: Split the atmosphere into vertical scenes with one parallax hero object and one caption rail per section.

items.implementationPrompt: Build a responsive website inspired by the Arte Collective poster "Cloud". Ignore the room mockup and frame; isolate the poster art, keep the palette around #2b626f, #e9e6e6, and #389eb5, use Wide Grotesk Display (approx) for the hero voice and Light Grotesk Text (approx) for metadata, build the page as a Playable Poster surface with Spatial Exhibition World mechanics, and stage the desktop hero as the full poster becomes the atmosphere, with title and texture layers drifting at different depths and slim orbital captions around the edges. Mobile fallback: Split the atmosphere into vertical scenes with one parallax hero object and one caption rail per section.
avoidIf: you need proof of a real live site instead of a poster-derived concept

items.title: Geometry refraction vol.1
items.description: Geometry refraction vol.1 is treated here as a poster-derived website concept. The source is the Arte Collective poster, but the design guidance describes how to turn the art into a live browser world with depth, motion, and mobile behavior.

worldSystems.name: Luxury Archive
worldSystems.reason: The poster reads like an object accessioned into a high-contrast digital chamber.

worldSystems.name: Fan Shrine
worldSystems.reason: The browser version should feel collectible and tactile rather than informational.

archetype.name: Spatial Exhibition World
archetype.reason: The poster wants to become one hero object inside a shallow 3D room with hotspot detail and object focus.

mechanics.interactionModel: Explode the full poster across the viewport: the headline becomes architecture, the background copy becomes the room, and the hardware collage becomes floating evidence layers that react to scroll, drag, and hover.
mechanics.mobileFallback: Reduce to one guided poster stage with swipe between viewpoints and a single expandable evidence rail under the hero object.

items.implementationPrompt: Build a responsive website inspired by the Arte Collective poster "Geometry refraction vol.1". Ignore the room mockup and frame; isolate the poster art, keep the palette around #0f0e0d, #e8e8e8, and #2c2826, use Ultra Condensed Grotesk Display (approx) for the hero voice and Bitmap Mono Caption (approx) for metadata, build the page as a Luxury Archive surface with Spatial Exhibition World mechanics, and stage the desktop hero as the full poster fills the room; title planes become architecture, copy becomes atmosphere, and the object collage separates into floating evidence layers. Mobile fallback: Reduce to one guided poster stage with swipe between viewpoints and a single expandable evidence rail under the hero object.
avoidIf: you need proof of a real live site instead of a poster-derived concept

items.title: Look at the sky
items.description: Look at the sky is treated here as a poster-derived website concept. The source is the Arte Collective poster, but the design guidance describes how to turn the art into a live browser world with depth, motion, and mobile behavior.

worldSystems.name: Playable Poster
worldSystems.reason: The poster already behaves like a campaign artifact and should stay singular on the web.

worldSystems.name: Luxury Archive
worldSystems.reason: The atmosphere needs enough structure to stay legible while drifting.

archetype.name: Spatial Exhibition World
archetype.reason: The poster becomes strongest as a layered atmospheric chamber with shallow depth, orbiting labels, and slow camera travel.

mechanics.interactionModel: Treat the full poster like a luminous atmospheric field; scroll advances chapters, pointer drift shifts parallax layers, and tap opens captions floating inside the composition.
mechanics.mobileFallback: Split the atmosphere into vertical scenes with one parallax hero object and one caption rail per section.

items.implementationPrompt: Build a responsive website inspired by the Arte Collective poster "Look at the sky". Ignore the room mockup and frame; isolate the poster art, keep the palette around #1f1f1f, #e6e6e6, and #d7c8ad, use Wide Grotesk Display (approx) for the hero voice and Light Grotesk Text (approx) for metadata, build the page as a Playable Poster surface with Spatial Exhibition World mechanics, and stage the desktop hero as the full poster becomes the atmosphere, with title and texture layers drifting at different depths and slim orbital captions around the edges. Mobile fallback: Split the atmosphere into vertical scenes with one parallax hero object and one caption rail per section.
avoidIf: you need proof of a real live site instead of a poster-derived concept

items.title: Mind's Expansion
items.description: Mind's Expansion is treated here as a poster-derived website concept. The source is the Arte Collective poster, but the design guidance describes how to turn the art into a live browser world with depth, motion, and mobile behavior.

worldSystems.name: Playable Poster
worldSystems.reason: The poster already behaves like a campaign artifact and should stay singular on the web.

worldSystems.name: Luxury Archive
worldSystems.reason: The atmosphere needs enough structure to stay legible while drifting.

archetype.name: Spatial Exhibition World
archetype.reason: The poster becomes strongest as a layered atmospheric chamber with shallow depth, orbiting labels, and slow camera travel.

mechanics.interactionModel: Treat the full poster like a luminous atmospheric field; scroll advances chapters, pointer drift shifts parallax layers, and tap opens captions floating inside the composition.
mechanics.mobileFallback: Split the atmosphere into vertical scenes with one parallax hero object and one caption rail per section.

items.implementationPrompt: Build a responsive website inspired by the Arte Collective poster "Mind's Expansion". Ignore the room mockup and frame; isolate the poster art, keep the palette around #131211, #e6e7e7, and #2d4585, use Wide Grotesk Display (approx) for the hero voice and Light Grotesk Text (approx) for metadata, build the page as a Playable Poster surface with Spatial Exhibition World mechanics, and stage the desktop hero as the full poster becomes the atmosphere, with title and texture layers drifting at different depths and slim orbital captions around the edges. Mobile fallback: Split the atmosphere into vertical scenes with one parallax hero object and one caption rail per section.
avoidIf: you need proof of a real live site instead of a poster-derived concept

archetype.name: Club Instrument
archetype.reason: The poster language wants to become a reactive field of signals, meters, panels, and pressure-sensitive motion.

mechanics.interactionModel: Turn the full poster into a responsive signal field: pointer movement shifts grids, tap reveals diagnostic panels, and drag scrubs the title, copy, and object fragments through layered depth planes.

items.implementationPrompt: Build a responsive website inspired by the Arte Collective poster "Neural Spectrum". Ignore the room mockup and frame; isolate the poster art, keep the palette around #16171c, #e6e6e6, and #3e2d5c, use Condensed Display Grotesk (approx) for the hero voice and Pixel Mono Console (approx) for metadata, build the page as a Club Instrument surface with Club Instrument mechanics, and stage the desktop hero as the whole poster becomes a tilted hud field, with title planes, copy grids, and object fragments separated into translucent depth rails. Mobile fallback: Collapse to one active hero slab, two stacked telemetry rails, and tap-to-cycle data states instead of precision hover behavior.

items.title: The Sun's Dying Soon - Blue

worldSystems.reason: The translation should feel edited, calm, and deliberately paced.

worldSystems.name: Playable Poster
worldSystems.reason: The page behaves like an authored text artifact rather than a neutral product shell.

archetype.name: Playable Poster
archetype.reason: The poster wants its text bars, negative space, and caption rhythm turned into the whole browser composition, not a generic scrolling layout.

mechanics.interactionModel: Turn the full poster into a typographic manifesto field: bars become interface slabs, key words become oversized live type, and small drag or scroll shifts the whole statement in depth.
mechanics.mobileFallback: Keep a single phrase stack, reveal one note at a time, and use swipe or tap to move between manifesto beats.

items.title: Type Saturn - Black
items.description: Type Saturn - Black is treated here as a poster-derived website concept. The source is the Arte Collective poster, but the design guidance describes how to turn the art into a live browser world with depth, motion, and mobile behavior.

worldSystems.name: Playable Poster
worldSystems.reason: The poster composition should stay bold and singular even after it becomes interactive.

worldSystems.name: Luxury Archive
worldSystems.reason: Structured labels and controlled negative space keep the result from becoming noise.

archetype.name: Playable Poster
archetype.reason: The poster wants to become an authored 3D type composition with a small number of high-conviction transforms.

mechanics.interactionModel: Extrude the full poster into a typographic chamber: drag rotates type planes, hover activates refraction layers, and scroll reveals staggered strata pulled from the composition.
mechanics.mobileFallback: Split the chamber into stacked scenes with one tilt response and one caption rail instead of maintaining full desktop depth.

items.implementationPrompt: Build a responsive website inspired by the Arte Collective poster "Type Saturn - Black". Ignore the room mockup and frame; isolate the poster art, keep the palette around #0d0c0b, #e7e7e7, and #12100f, use Extended Geometric Sans (approx) for the hero voice and Condensed Label Sans (approx) for metadata, build the page as a Playable Poster surface with Playable Poster mechanics, and stage the desktop hero as the full poster becomes a field of duplicated type planes, offset in z, with one narrow refraction rail cutting through the scene. Mobile fallback: Split the chamber into stacked scenes with one tilt response and one caption rail instead of maintaining full desktop depth.
avoidIf: you need proof of a real live site instead of a poster-derived concept

items.title: Urban Space Army - Agriculture vol.1

worldSystems.name: Club Instrument
worldSystems.reason: The web version should feel like a responsive system, not a static poster pinned to the wall.

worldSystems.name: Playable Poster
worldSystems.reason: Oversized type and one clear ritual keep the poster energy intact.

archetype.name: Club Instrument
archetype.reason: The poster language wants to become a reactive field of signals, meters, panels, and pressure-sensitive motion.

mechanics.interactionModel: Turn the full poster into a responsive signal field: pointer movement shifts grids, tap reveals diagnostic panels, and drag scrubs the title, copy, and object fragments through layered depth planes.
mechanics.mobileFallback: Collapse to one active hero slab, two stacked telemetry rails, and tap-to-cycle data states instead of precision hover behavior.

items.implementationPrompt: Build a responsive website inspired by the Arte Collective poster "Urban Space Army - Agriculture vol.1". Ignore the room mockup and frame; isolate the poster art, keep the palette around #1c281a, #e6e6e6, and #009a4a, use Condensed Display Grotesk (approx) for the hero voice and Pixel Mono Console (approx) for metadata, build the page as a Club Instrument surface with Club Instrument mechanics, and stage the desktop hero as the whole poster becomes a tilted hud field, with title planes, copy grids, and object fragments separated into translucent depth rails. Mobile fallback: Collapse to one active hero slab, two stacked telemetry rails, and tap-to-cycle data states instead of precision hover behavior.

you need dense productivity UI
